label: BugSnag
description: Monitor, triage, and resolve application errors and crashes from BugSnag (SmartBear).

form.title: Connect BugSnag
form.subtitle: Paste your personal auth token to give Anton access to your BugSnag projects and error data.

methods.label: Personal Auth Token
methods.description: Paste a personal auth token from your BugSnag account settings. The fastest and simplest way to connect — no OAuth app setup required.
methods.how_to:
## Generate a BugSnag Personal Auth Token

BugSnag issues per-user personal auth tokens that grant API access scoped to your account's permissions.

### Steps

1. Log in to [BugSnag](https://app.bugsnag.com).
2. Click your avatar or name in the top-right corner and choose **My Account**.
3. Scroll to **Settings → Personal auth tokens**.
4. Click **Generate new token**, give it a descriptive name like `Anton`, and confirm.
5. Copy the token immediately — BugSnag only shows it once.
6. Paste the token into the **Personal Auth Token** field below.

### Notes

- The token inherits your account's organization and project permissions. If you need access to a specific organization, make sure your account is a member.
- If you lose the token, generate a new one from the same page and update it in Anton.
- Revoking the token in BugSnag will immediately break Anton's connection — just paste a new one to restore it.

fields.name: personal_auth_token
fields.label: Personal Auth Token
fields.description: Found in BugSnag → My Account → Settings → Personal auth tokens. Shown only once on creation.

fields.name: organization_slug
fields.label: Organization Slug
fields.description: Optional. The slug for your BugSnag organization (visible in the URL when viewing your org). Leave blank to use the first organization associated with your token.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (BugSnag App)
methods.description: Authorize via BugSnag's OAuth 2.0 flow using your own BugSnag OAuth application. Requires creating an OAuth app in BugSnag's developer settings.
methods.how_to:
## OAuth 2.0 via BugSnag OAuth App

This method requires you to create a BugSnag OAuth application and supply its credentials. Use the **Personal Auth Token** method unless you specifically need OAuth.

### Steps

1. Log in to [BugSnag](https://app.bugsnag.com) and navigate to your **Organization Settings**.
2. Find the **Integrations** or **OAuth Applications** section and create a new OAuth application.
3. Set the redirect URI to `http://127.0.0.1` (or the local redirect Anton uses).
4. Note the generated **Client ID** and **Client Secret**.
5. Enter them in the fields below and click **Submit** to launch the browser-based consent flow.

### Notes

- The user authorizing the flow must have appropriate organization permissions in BugSnag.
- If BugSnag's OAuth application management is not available on your plan, use the Personal Auth Token method instead.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: From your BugSnag OAuth application settings under Organization Settings.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: Paired with the Client ID from your BugSnag OAuth application.
